_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to identify new indicators of compromise through anomalous behavior in log entries.
reference_document_element_description: Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, blocker escalation, and closure verification with evidence.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-S-001 (DQ, cosine: 0.403), RC-S-005 (RC, cosine: 0.394).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.502). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, anomalous, behavior, compromise, entries, identify] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, blocker, closure, compliance, data, escalation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.873, R=0.859). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DCWF element and 15-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (87.4%). "Ability to identify new indicators of compromise through anomalous behavior in l" and "Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, bl" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.405 < STS 0.502 (gap -0.097). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, anomalous, behavior] and [activities, blocker, closure] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, anomalous, behavior, compromise, entries, identify]. WIDAI terms: [activities, blocker, closure, compliance, data, escalation].
